title: Graphing basic inequalities
description: Identify graphs of inequalities like x>8 or x<-4.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the graph of the inequality  $x>-4$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The inequality $x>-4$  is read as $x$ is greater than $-4$.
So, we need to graph all values that are greater than $-4$.

**The answer:**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/052d2a0ad7e0ed0ebced52ff22605478f0cdb0e0)

choices.content: ![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at negative 4. A line running to the left starts at negative 4 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/889b8a05a19f7dfc62e3846d4362eccbacb58931)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. A closed point is plotted at negative 4.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c4d51ba5c9fbfb5590f3241553f36fbead0e2d4a)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at 4. A line running to the left starts at 4 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3bcf9457a8ccfcc572a451a55eb860060c5ad671)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at negative 4. A line running to the right starts at negative 4 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/052d2a0ad7e0ed0ebced52ff22605478f0cdb0e0)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the graph of the inequality  $-1>x$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $-1>x$  is read as $-1$ is greater than $x$.  

So, $x$ must be less than $-1$.
So, we need to graph all values that are less than $-1$.

**The answer:**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a126afb3e7bf25f070092eae621d17f0e4df03d)

choices.content: ![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at 1. A line running to the right starts at 1 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9388dfaf978b768d0aad28dcbc2c547a141db518)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at negative 1. A line running to the right starts at negative 1 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6cb0017a77a27610e1b9255c8f26a339db58f50d)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at 1. A line running to the left starts at 1 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3977545e176131825f917115c7d6f358e0b4266b)

![A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at negative 1. A line running to the left starts at negative 1 and continues.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a126afb3e7bf25f070092eae621d17f0e4df03d)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the graph of the inequality  $5<x$.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $5<x$  is read as $5$ is less than $x$.  

So, $x$ must be greater than $5$.
So, we need to graph all values that are greater than $5$.

**The answer:**


[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the inequality that represents the following graph.**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The arrow points to all values greater than $2$.
So, the inequality represents everything **greater than $2$.**

**The answer:**

$2<x$

options.alt: A number line from negative 5 to 5 with evenly spaced tick marks in increments of 1. An open point is plotted at 2. A line running to the right starts at 2 and continues.
